task_completion.description: Checks if the product title and 'Preliminary' status are at the top of the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'SE-9042 Low-Power IoT Transceiver' and 'Preliminary' at the very top of the document page. They should span the full width of the page, not constrained to a narrow column.

task_completion.description: Checks if the Description, Features, and Applications sections are formatted in a two-column layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text under Description, Features, and Applications to be split into two distinct vertical columns side-by-side on the page. The top ruler in OpenOffice Writer should also indicate a two-column split.

task_completion.description: Checks if the specific technical values from the JSON are incorporated into the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the specific strings '-148 dBm', '4.6 mA', and '0.1 kbps' within the text or lists in the Features or Description sections.

task_completion.description: Checks if a table is used for the Electrical Characteristics section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a structured grid/table containing electrical characteristics data. There should be visible table borders or cell alignments distinct from regular paragraph text.

task_completion.description: Checks if the required confidential text is placed in the document footer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the bottom margin of the page (the footer area) for the exact text 'SiliconEdge Solutions - Confidential'.

task_completion.description: Checks if the document is saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the title bar of the OpenOffice Writer window or a 'Save As' dialog box to confirm the file is named 'SE9042_Datasheet.odt'.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent used the Apache OpenOffice Writer GUI to create the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the Apache OpenOffice Writer application interface (menus, toolbars, page layout view) in the screenshots, rather than terminal commands generating the .odt file via scripts.

integrity.description: Verifies the two-column layout was created using actual column formatting tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the ruler at the top of the document showing column margins/gutters, or the 'Columns' formatting dialog box being used, rather than the agent just using tabs or spaces to fake columns.

integrity.description: Verifies the Electrical Characteristics section is a real table.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for table gridlines, the table toolbar, or the 'Insert Table' dialog being used, rather than text manually aligned with spaces or tabs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The document must be created in Apache OpenOffice Writer. The top of the document must include 'SE-9042', 'Low-Power IoT Transceiver', and 'Preliminary' in a full-width layout. The main body (Description, Features, Applications) must use a 2-column layout. The document must include the exact values: '-148 dBm', '4.6 mA', and '0.1 kbps'. A table must be inserted for Electrical Characteristics. The footer must contain exactly 'SiliconEdge Solutions - Confidential'. The final file must be saved as 'SE9042_Datasheet.odt'.
